summary: The session focused on unique wedding traditions in the Guangxi region of China, including the "Picking up the bride" ceremony, which involves the groom overcoming challenges to reach the bride. Additional customs discussed included "Sitting on the bridal bed," the "Six Dragon and Phoenix Ball," and the "Zuo Tang" ceremony, all of which are rooted in ancient Chinese culture and symbolize good luck and prosperity.
facts: The "Picking up the bride" ceremony in Guangxi involves the groom and his groomsmen overcoming various challenges to reach the bride's home and take her to the wedding venue. During the "Picking up the bride" ceremony, the groom must perform tasks such as solving puzzles or passing through flower-decorated gates to prove his worthiness. In Guangxi, the "Sitting on the bridal bed" custom involves the bride and groom picking up nuts, seeds, and soybeans thrown on the bed for good luck. The "Six Dragon and Phoenix Ball" custom in Guangxi features six red China balls with dragon and phoenix motifs hung above the bridal bed to bring good luck and prosperity. The "Zuo Tang" ceremony in Guangxi allows family and friends to visit the bride and groom in the bridal chamber to offer congratulations and gifts. The "Sitting on the bridal bed" custom and the "Six Dragon and Phoenix Ball" tradition originated from the Tang Dynasty. The "Zuo Tang" ceremony is believed to have originated in the Ming Dynasty as a way for guests to congratulate the couple. Red is a prominent color used in Chinese weddings, symbolizing good luck and happiness. These wedding customs in Guangxi are deeply embedded in Chinese culture and have been practiced for generations.
